# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder_30b)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on shared runners and executes custom build scripts from repository. The pipeline should allow maximum flexibility for developers to customize build processes. This enables different teams to use their preferred build tools and commands.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - build
  - test
  - deploy

variables:
  CUSTOM_SCRIPTS_PATH: ".gitlab/custom-scripts"
  DEFAULT_BUILD_SCRIPT: "build.sh"

before_script:
  - mkdir -p $CUSTOM_SCRIPTS_PATH
  - chmod +x $CUSTOM_SCRIPTS_PATH/*

build:
  stage: build
  script:
    - |
      if [ -f "$CUSTOM_SCRIPTS_PATH/$DEFAULT_BUILD_SCRIPT" ]; then
        $CUSTOM_SCRIPTS_PATH/$DEFAULT_BUILD_SCRIPT
      else
        echo "No custom build script found, using default build process"
        # Default build commands can be added here
      fi
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - build/
    expire_in: 1 week

test:
  stage: test
  script:
    - |
      if [ -f "$CUSTOM_SCRIPTS_PATH/test.sh" ]; then
        $CUSTOM_SCRIPTS_PATH/test.sh
      else
        echo "No custom test script found, skipping tests"
      fi

deploy:
  stage: deploy
  script:
    - |
      if [ -f "$CUSTOM_SCRIPTS_PATH/deploy.sh" ]; then
        $CUSTOM_SCRIPTS_PATH/deploy.sh
      else
        echo "No custom deploy script found, using default deployment"
      fi
  only:
    - main
    - master

workflow:
  rules:
    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"
    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "push"
    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "web"
